payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new because it is the newer active memory for Sam in the Goldman context. mem_old was filtered out because it is linked as superseded, so it is not considered current recallable state. This prevents presenting an outdated fact as current when a newer replacement exists for the same entity/context.
payload.decision: return mem_new as the current recall result and exclude mem_old because mem_old is superseded by a newer conflicting memory about the same subject/context

entity_resolution.reason: Both memories refer to the same person, Sam, within the Goldman context, so conflict handling is applied on the same entity/context pair.

memory_cards.statement: mem_new is the active memory for Sam in the Goldman context and is eligible for recall.
mem_old refers to the same subject/context but is not returned because it has been superseded by mem_new.

details: Matched both records to the same entity/context pair: Sam + Goldman.
Detected a supersession relationship in which mem_old is replaced by mem_new.
Applied recall rule to return only the current non-superseded memory.
Excluded mem_old to avoid surfacing stale information as current.

repair_options: Inspect the superseded_by or replacement link between mem_old and mem_new.
Verify both memories resolve to the same Sam and the same Goldman context.
Check timestamps/version ordering to confirm mem_new is newer than mem_old.
If mem_old should still be visible, remove or revise the supersession status rather than treating both as current.
